prodState.label: Producer-states & state-owned miners
prodState.desc: Governments that mine or refine directly (Codelco, Chinese REE groups, PT Timah, Nornickel).

major.label: Private diversified mining majors
major.desc: Investor-owned producers — BHP, Rio Tinto, Glencore, Freeport, Anglo American.

trader.label: Commodity traders & merchants
trader.desc: Firms that market and move physical metal — Glencore (trading arm), Trafigura.

financial.label: Financial institutions
financial.desc: Asset managers, ETF issuers, bullion banks — BlackRock, State Street, Sprott, JPMorgan.

centralGov.label: Central banks & treasuries
centralGov.desc: States acting as reserve buyers, stockpilers, financiers — PBoC, US DLA, defense stockpiles.

endUser.label: Large end-users (vertical integrators)
endUser.desc: Battery, auto, magnet, electronics firms securing supply upstream — CATL, Toyota, LG.

physical.label: Physical supply
physical.desc: Controlling how much metal exists — mining, capacity expansion/deferral, refining bottleneck.

tradePolicy.label: Trade & policy
tradePolicy.desc: Export controls, tariffs, sanctions, licensing — changes where metal can go, not how much exists.

accumulate.label: Strategic accumulation
accumulate.desc: Buying and holding to remove metal from the market — reserves, national stockpiles.

financial.label: Financial positioning
financial.desc: Moving price via futures, ETFs and paper claims without holding physical metal.

offtake.label: Offtake & contracts
offtake.desc: Locking future supply through long-term purchase agreements and equity stakes in producers.

reading.gold: Movers cluster in the financial and accumulation columns — central banks and ETF issuers, not miners, set the price. A monetary asset.
reading.silver: Split personality: financial positioning dominates, but physical supply from mining majors matters more than for gold.
reading.platinum: Physical-supply column dominates — a handful of South African majors. Concentrated operational and country risk.
reading.palladium: Physical supply plus a real trade-policy weight from Russia. Producer concentration is the core risk.
reading.rhodium: Almost purely a physical-supply story, and a tiny one — few producers, thin market, extreme price sensitivity.
reading.lithium: Mining majors plus aggressive end-user offtake — battery makers lock up supply by contract. Watch the offtake column.
reading.cobalt: Unusually broad: majors, traders (Glencore), and end-users all pull hard. Trader influence is a distinctive feature.
reading.nickel: State producers (Indonesia, Russia) plus majors. Trade policy carries real weight via Indonesian export rules.
reading.copper: Broad and liquid — state and private producers plus genuine financial positioning on deep futures markets.
reading.graphite: Trade-policy column lights up: Chinese export licensing is a primary mover, alongside end-user offtake.
reading.neodymium: Dominated by the state-producer and trade-policy columns — China's REE groups and Ministry of Commerce.
reading.dysprosium: Most concentrated profile on the board — state production and trade policy, with heavy-REE supply even tighter than light REEs.
reading.gallium: Trade-policy column is decisive — a near-pure China policy instrument since the 2023-24 export controls.
reading.germanium: Same shape as gallium: a byproduct metal turned policy lever through Chinese export licensing.
reading.tin: State producers plus traders, with modest financial positioning — a quiet metal that is a direct bet on electronics solder.
